steps.text: Translate the word description: $f$ gives the product of $x$ and a number that is $91$ more than $x$. The second factor is $x + 91$.
Expand by distributing: $f(x) = x \cdot x + x \cdot 91 = x^2 + 91x$.

Evaluate all choices:
  • A: $f(x) = x^2 + x + 91$ — has an extra $+91$ constant and uses $+x$ instead of $+91x$. This is not the product of $x$ and $x+91$. Eliminated.
  • B: $f(x) = x^2 + 91$ — missing the $91x$ term; this would equal $x^2$ plus a constant, not a product of two linear expressions in $x$. Eliminated.
  • C: $f(x) = x^2 + 91x$ — matches $x(x+91)$ exactly. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: $f(x) = x^2 + 91x + 91$ — has an extra constant $+91$ that doesn"t appear in the product. Eliminated.
